_meta.description: ICD-10 treatment reference used by the ICD-10 Selector Agent to enrich clinical context before SOAP report generation. Each entry can be extended by specialty or per-clinic customisation — see the 'specialty' field.
_meta.usage: Add entries freely. The selector agent reads code, name, specialty, symptoms_keywords, drugs, protocol, contraindications, and notes.
_meta.extend_instructions: To add specialty-specific entries, set 'specialty' to the relevant specialty name (e.g. 'cardiology', 'endocrinology'). The repository supports filtering by specialty.

conditions.name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications
conditions.protocol: Lifestyle modification (diet + exercise) for 3 months first. Start Metformin if HbA1c > 6.5% or fasting glucose > 7.0 mmol/L. Add second agent if target not reached after 3 months.
conditions.contraindications: Hold Metformin if eGFR < 30 mL/min. Avoid sulfonylureas in severe renal impairment.
conditions.notes: Monitor HbA1c every 3 months until stable, then every 6 months. Check renal function annually.

conditions.name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia
conditions.protocol: Immediate glucose control. Consider insulin if HbA1c > 9% or symptomatic hyperglycaemia. Adjust oral agents after stabilisation.
conditions.contraindications: Avoid SGLT-2 inhibitors during acute illness.
conditions.notes: Check ketones. Educate patient on sick-day rules.

conditions.name: Essential (primary) hypertension
conditions.protocol: Target BP < 130/80 mmHg. Start with CCB or ACEi/ARB monotherapy. Combine if target not reached in 4 weeks. Add low-dose thiazide as third agent.
conditions.contraindications: ACEi/ARB contraindicated in pregnancy and bilateral renal artery stenosis. Avoid beta-blockers as first-line unless co-existing angina or heart failure.
conditions.notes: Check renal function and electrolytes 1–2 weeks after starting ACEi/ARB. Lifestyle: reduce salt, alcohol, weight.

conditions.name: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery without angina pectoris
conditions.protocol: All patients: antiplatelet + high-intensity statin. Add beta-blocker if EF < 40%. ACEi if EF < 40% or hypertension. Lifestyle modification mandatory.
conditions.contraindications: Aspirin contraindicated if active peptic ulcer. Statin: monitor liver enzymes.
conditions.notes: LDL target < 1.8 mmol/L. Annual cardiology review. Refer for stress test or angiography if symptomatic.

conditions.name: Acute upper respiratory infection, unspecified
conditions.protocol: Supportive treatment. No antibiotics unless bacterial superinfection suspected. Adequate hydration and rest.
conditions.contraindications: Avoid aspirin in children (Reye's syndrome risk). Do not use cough suppressants in children under 6.
conditions.notes: Educate patient: viral illness, usually self-limiting 7–10 days. Return if fever > 38.5°C persists > 3 days.

conditions.name: Pneumonia, unspecified organism
drugs: Azithromycin 500 mg OD x5 days (atypical coverage)
conditions.protocol: Assess severity with CURB-65. Score 0–1: oral outpatient. Score 2+: consider hospitalisation. Cover atypicals if CURB-65 ≥ 2 or young adult.
conditions.contraindications: Penicillin allergy: use azithromycin or levofloxacin.
conditions.notes: Obtain sputum culture before antibiotics if possible. Reassess at 48–72 hours. Pneumococcal vaccine counselling.

conditions.name: Asthma, unspecified
drugs: Salbutamol 100 mcg inhaler 2 puffs Q4–6H PRN (SABA, reliever)
Prednisolone 40 mg OD x5 days (acute exacerbation)
conditions.protocol: Step 1 (mild intermittent): SABA PRN only. Step 2 (mild persistent): add low-dose ICS. Step 3: ICS + LABA. Acute severe: SABA + systemic steroid.
conditions.contraindications: Avoid non-selective beta-blockers. NSAIDs in aspirin-sensitive asthma.
conditions.notes: Assess inhaler technique at every visit. Peak flow monitoring. Annual spirometry.

conditions.name: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with acute exacerbation
symptoms_keywords: bệnh phổi tắc nghẽn mạn tính
drugs: Salbutamol 2.5 mg nebuliser Q20 min x3 (acute)
Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125 mg BD x5 days (if purulent sputum)
conditions.protocol: Acute: controlled O2 (SpO2 88–92%), bronchodilators, systemic steroids, antibiotics if purulent sputum. Long-term: LAMA ± LABA ± ICS depending on GOLD stage.
conditions.contraindications: High-flow O2 may suppress respiratory drive in chronic hypercapnia.
conditions.notes: Smoking cessation is the single most important intervention. Pulmonary rehab referral. Influenza and pneumococcal vaccination.

conditions.name: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease with oesophagitis
drugs: Omeprazole 20–40 mg OD before breakfast (PPI, 4–8 weeks)
Gaviscon 10 mL TDS after meals PRN (antacid/alginate)
conditions.protocol: Lifestyle first: elevate head of bed, avoid triggers (spicy food, coffee, alcohol, late meals). PPI 4–8 weeks for erosive oesophagitis. Maintenance PPI if relapse.
conditions.contraindications: Long-term PPI: risk of hypomagnesaemia, C. diff, bone density reduction. Review need annually.
conditions.notes: Alarm features (dysphagia, weight loss, haematemesis): urgent endoscopy. H. pylori test if dyspepsia.

conditions.name: Gastric ulcer, unspecified
drugs: Omeprazole 40 mg BD (PPI, high dose x4–8 weeks)
Amoxicillin 1 g BD + Clarithromycin 500 mg BD x7 days (H. pylori eradication — triple therapy)
Bismuth subcitrate 120 mg QDS (quadruple therapy add-on)
conditions.protocol: Test for H. pylori (urea breath test or stool antigen). If positive: triple or quadruple eradication therapy. PPI 4–8 weeks. Stop NSAIDs if possible.
conditions.contraindications: Clarithromycin resistance in some regions — check local antibiogram.
conditions.notes: Confirm ulcer healing by endoscopy at 6–8 weeks for gastric ulcer (cancer risk). Avoid NSAIDs, steroids, alcohol.

conditions.name: Urinary tract infection, site not specified
drugs: Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole 960 mg BD x3 days (uncomplicated female)
Nitrofurantoin 100 mg BD x5–7 days (uncomplicated female)
Ciprofloxacin 500 mg BD x3 days (uncomplicated, fluoroquinolone)
conditions.protocol: Uncomplicated female: oral antibiotics x3 days. Complicated or male: x7–14 days. Pyelonephritis: x10–14 days, consider IV if vomiting.
conditions.contraindications: Nitrofurantoin: avoid if eGFR < 30. Fluoroquinolones: avoid in pregnancy.
conditions.notes: Urine M/C/S before antibiotics if complicated. Increase fluid intake. Recurrent UTI (≥3/year): consider prophylaxis.

conditions.name: Low back pain
drugs: Ibuprofen 400 mg TDS with food (NSAID, short course ≤7 days)
conditions.protocol: Non-specific LBP: stay active, avoid bed rest > 2 days. First-line: paracetamol ± NSAID short course. Physiotherapy referral if > 4 weeks. Red flags: urgent imaging.
conditions.contraindications: NSAIDs: avoid in peptic ulcer, renal impairment, cardiovascular disease. Opioids: avoid as first-line.
conditions.notes: Red flags: fever, weight loss, history of cancer, saddle anaesthesia, bladder/bowel dysfunction — urgent MRI.

conditions.name: Rheumatoid arthritis, unspecified
drugs: Ibuprofen 400 mg TDS (NSAID for symptom relief)
Folic acid 5 mg weekly (to reduce Methotrexate side-effects)
conditions.protocol: Treat-to-target: aim for DAS28 remission. Start Methotrexate early. Add hydroxychloroquine or sulfasalazine for combination DMARD. Biologic (anti-TNF) if inadequate response to 2 DMARDs.
conditions.contraindications: Methotrexate: avoid in pregnancy, significant renal/hepatic impairment, or active infection.
conditions.notes: Monitor FBC, LFT, renal function every 3 months. Annual CXR if on Methotrexate. Vaccinations before biologic initiation.

conditions.name: Major depressive episode, unspecified
conditions.protocol: Start SSRI at low dose, titrate after 2 weeks. Full therapeutic effect at 4–6 weeks. Continue ≥6 months after remission for first episode. Psychotherapy (CBT) alongside medication.
conditions.contraindications: Do not combine SSRIs with MAOIs (serotonin syndrome). QTc prolongation risk with citalopram at high doses.
conditions.notes: Assess suicide risk at every visit. Monitor for activation symptoms in first 2 weeks. Refer to psychiatrist if severe, psychotic features, or treatment-resistant.

conditions.name: Generalised anxiety disorder
conditions.protocol: CBT is first-line. Pharmacotherapy: SSRI/SNRI as first-line, onset 4–6 weeks. Short-term benzodiazepine for severe acute distress only. Avoid long-term benzodiazepine.
conditions.contraindications: Benzodiazepines: dependence risk, avoid in history of substance abuse.
conditions.notes: Screen for co-existing depression. Lifestyle: exercise, sleep hygiene, caffeine reduction.

conditions.name: Hypothyroidism, unspecified
drugs: Levothyroxine 25–200 mcg OD (T4 replacement, fasting, 30 min before food)
conditions.protocol: Start low dose (25–50 mcg in elderly/cardiac). Titrate every 4–6 weeks based on TSH. Target TSH 0.5–2.5 mIU/L for most patients.
conditions.contraindications: Caution in ischaemic heart disease — start very low dose. Excess levothyroxine risks atrial fibrillation and osteoporosis.
conditions.notes: Take on empty stomach. Check TSH every 6 weeks until stable, then annually. Many drug interactions (calcium, iron, PPI reduce absorption).

conditions.name: Thyrotoxicosis, unspecified
drugs: Propylthiouracil 100–200 mg TDS (alternative, preferred in pregnancy 1st trimester)
Propranolol 40 mg TDS (symptom control — palpitations, tremor)
conditions.protocol: Start antithyroid drug. Add beta-blocker for symptomatic control. Reassess thyroid function every 4–6 weeks. Consider radioiodine or surgery after 12–18 months.
conditions.contraindications: PTU: hepatotoxicity risk — monitor LFT. Radioiodine: contraindicated in pregnancy.
conditions.notes: Monitor FBC (agranulocytosis risk with carbimazole — advise patient to report sore throat/fever immediately). Graves' disease: ophthalmic review.

conditions.name: Chronic kidney disease, stage 3
drugs: Enalapril 5–20 mg OD (ACEi — reduces proteinuria, slows progression)
Amlodipine 5–10 mg OD (BP control if ACEi insufficient)
Sodium bicarbonate 500–1000 mg BD (correct metabolic acidosis if HCO3 < 22)
Erythropoietin SC (if Hb < 100 g/L and iron replete)
Ferrous sulphate 200 mg BD (if iron deficient)
conditions.protocol: BP target < 130/80 mmHg. ACEi/ARB to reduce proteinuria. Correct anaemia, acidosis, hyperphosphataemia. Avoid nephrotoxins (NSAIDs, contrast, aminoglycosides). Referral to nephrology if eGFR < 30.
conditions.contraindications: NSAIDs: strictly avoid. Metformin: hold if eGFR < 30. Gadolinium contrast: caution if eGFR < 30.
conditions.notes: Monitor eGFR, electrolytes, bicarbonate every 3–6 months. Dietary advice: limit protein, phosphate, potassium if advanced CKD.

conditions.name: Heart failure, unspecified
drugs: Enalapril 2.5–20 mg BD (ACEi — mortality benefit HFrEF)
Spironolactone 25–50 mg OD (MRA — add if EF ≤ 35%)
Sacubitril/Valsartan 24/26–97/103 mg BD (ARNI — if ACEi tolerated)
conditions.protocol: HFrEF (EF < 40%): ACEi + beta-blocker + MRA (the three pillars). Decongestion with diuretic. ARNI can replace ACEi. HFpEF: treat underlying cause and symptoms.
conditions.contraindications: ACEi + ARB combination: avoid. Beta-blocker: do not start in acute decompensation.
conditions.notes: Daily weight monitoring. Fluid restriction 1.5 L/day if severe. Cardiac rehab referral. SGLT-2 inhibitors (dapagliflozin) now first-line for HFrEF.

conditions.name: Unspecified atrial fibrillation
drugs: Apixaban 5 mg BD (NOAC — stroke prevention, preferred)
Warfarin 2–10 mg OD (VKA — if NOAC unavailable, target INR 2–3)
conditions.protocol: CHA₂DS₂-VASc score: anticoagulate if ≥ 1 (male) or ≥ 2 (female). Rate control target HR < 110 bpm at rest. Rhythm control: cardioversion ± antiarrhythmic if symptomatic.
conditions.contraindications: Warfarin: many drug interactions, monitor INR closely. NOACs: reduce dose if renal impairment.
conditions.notes: HAS-BLED score to assess bleeding risk. Do not withhold anticoagulation due to fall risk alone. Thyroid function, echo recommended at diagnosis.

conditions.name: Migraine, unspecified
conditions.protocol: Acute: take medication early in attack. Mild-moderate: NSAID ± antiemetic. Severe: triptan. Prophylaxis if ≥ 4 attacks/month or disabling attacks.
conditions.contraindications: Triptans: contraindicated in ischaemic heart disease, uncontrolled hypertension, basilar migraine. Avoid opioids for migraine.
conditions.notes: Headache diary. Identify and avoid triggers. Limit analgesic use to ≤ 10 days/month (medication overuse headache).

conditions.name: Epilepsy, unspecified
drugs: Valproate 400–2000 mg/day divided BD (broad-spectrum, generalised epilepsy)
Levetiracetam 500–3000 mg/day divided BD (adjunct or monotherapy)
conditions.protocol: Start monotherapy, titrate slowly. Switch if inadequate seizure control. Two AEDs have failed: refer to epilepsy specialist. Driving restrictions apply.
conditions.contraindications: Valproate: avoid in women of childbearing age (teratogenicity — neural tube defects). Carbamazepine: SJS risk in HLA-B*1502 (Asian populations — screen before use).
conditions.notes: Never stop AED abruptly. Monitor levels for phenytoin, carbamazepine, valproate. Women: folic acid 5 mg OD if considering pregnancy.

conditions.name: Atopic dermatitis, unspecified
drugs: Betamethasone 0.1% cream BD (moderate TCS, body, short course)
Cetirizine 10 mg OD (antihistamine — sedating: chlorphenamine 4 mg nocte if severe itch)
Tacrolimus 0.03–0.1% ointment BD (topical calcineurin inhibitor, steroid-sparing)
Emollient (e.g. white soft paraffin) applied TDS–QDS
conditions.protocol: Emollient as base treatment (every day, every patient). Topical corticosteroid for flares. Tacrolimus for face, flexures, and steroid-sparing. Antihistamine for itch/sleep. Identify and avoid triggers.
conditions.contraindications: Potent TCS: avoid on face, avoid in children for prolonged periods (skin atrophy).
conditions.notes: Step-up if uncontrolled: dupilumab (biologic) for moderate-severe adult AD. Skin infection screening (S. aureus).

conditions.name: Viral infection, unspecified
drugs: Ibuprofen 400 mg Q8H PRN (if paracetamol insufficient, not dengue)
conditions.protocol: Supportive care: rest, hydration, antipyretics. No antibiotics unless bacterial co-infection confirmed. Monitor for deterioration.
conditions.contraindications: Avoid ibuprofen/NSAIDs in suspected dengue (bleeding risk). Aspirin in children.
conditions.notes: Consider dengue, influenza, COVID-19 in febrile patient. Report notifiable diseases. Safety-net: return if rash, bleeding, severe headache, neck stiffness.

conditions.name: Dengue fever (classical dengue)
drugs: Paracetamol 500–1000 mg Q6H PRN (ONLY — no NSAIDs/aspirin)
IV normal saline (if severe dehydration or haemodynamic instability)
conditions.protocol: Febrile phase (days 1–3): paracetamol only, oral hydration, monitoring. Critical phase (days 4–6): watch for plasma leakage, thrombocytopenia — hospitalise if platelets < 100 000 or warning signs. Recovery phase: reabsorption, avoid fluid overload.
conditions.contraindications: STRICTLY no ibuprofen, aspirin, or NSAIDs (severe bleeding risk). No intramuscular injections.
conditions.notes: Warning signs: abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, bleeding, lethargy, rapid deterioration → emergency admission. Notify health authority (notifiable disease).

conditions.name: Acute pharyngitis, unspecified
drugs: Phenoxymethylpenicillin 500 mg QDS x10 days (strep pharyngitis)
Amoxicillin 500 mg TDS x10 days (strep, alternative)
conditions.protocol: Viral: symptomatic only. Bacterial (Group A strep — Centor ≥ 3 or rapid antigen positive): penicillin x10 days. Do not use antibiotics for viral pharyngitis.
conditions.contraindications: Amoxicillin: avoid if EBV suspected (infective mononucleosis) — causes rash.
conditions.notes: Centor criteria: tonsillar exudate, tender anterior cervical lymphadenopathy, fever, absence of cough. Rapid strep test if available.

conditions.name: Diverticular disease of large intestine without perforation or abscess
drugs: Paracetamol 1 g Q6H (analgesia — avoid opioids if possible)
Metronidazole 400 mg TDS x7–10 days (antibiotic for diverticulitis)
Ciprofloxacin 500 mg BD x7–10 days (in combination with metronidazole)
conditions.protocol: Uncomplicated diverticulitis (mild): oral antibiotics outpatient x7–10 days + liquid diet. Complicated (abscess, perforation, peritonitis): hospitalise, IV antibiotics, surgical review.
conditions.contraindications: Avoid morphine/opioids if possible (increase intraluminal pressure).
conditions.notes: High-fibre diet for prevention. CT abdomen/pelvis for diagnosis. Colonoscopy 6–8 weeks after resolution to exclude malignancy.

conditions.name: Cerebral infarction, unspecified
drugs: Aspirin 300 mg stat then 75–100 mg OD (antiplatelet)
Atorvastatin 40–80 mg OD (statin — all ischaemic stroke)
Enalapril 5–10 mg OD (BP control — start 24–48 h after acute stroke)
Alteplase IV 0.9 mg/kg (thrombolysis — within 4.5 h of onset, if eligible)
conditions.protocol: FAST: Face, Arms, Speech, Time. Acute: thrombolysis if within 4.5 h and no contraindications. Secondary prevention: antiplatelet + statin + BP control. AF-related stroke: anticoagulation.
conditions.contraindications: Thrombolysis: contraindicated if recent surgery, haemorrhagic stroke, uncontrolled BP > 185/110.
conditions.notes: Urgent brain CT (to exclude haemorrhage before thrombolysis). Stroke unit admission. Multidisciplinary rehab: physiotherapy, speech therapy, occupational therapy.

conditions.name: Malignant neoplasm of bronchus and lung, unspecified
drugs: Morphine SR 10–30 mg BD (pain and dyspnoea palliation)
conditions.protocol: Staging (TNM) guides treatment. Stage I–II: surgery if resectable. Stage III: concurrent chemoradiation. Stage IV: systemic therapy based on molecular profile (EGFR, ALK, PD-L1). Always tissue biopsy before treatment.
conditions.contraindications: Immunotherapy: contraindicated in active autoimmune disease on immunosuppressants.
conditions.notes: Refer to multidisciplinary tumour board. Smoking cessation. Molecular profiling mandatory before starting targeted therapy. Palliative care integration early.

conditions.name: Hyperlipidaemia, unspecified
drugs: Ezetimibe 10 mg OD (add-on if LDL target not reached)
conditions.protocol: Risk stratification first. High CV risk (10-year risk > 10% or established CVD): target LDL < 1.8 mmol/L. Moderate risk: < 2.6 mmol/L. Start high-intensity statin + lifestyle.
conditions.contraindications: Statins: avoid in pregnancy. Monitor CK if myalgia. Fibrates + statin: myopathy risk — use cautiously.
conditions.notes: Diet: reduce saturated fat, trans fat. Exercise. Check LFT and CK at baseline and at 3 months. Annual lipid panel once stable.

conditions.name: Gout, unspecified
drugs: Indomethacin 50 mg TDS (NSAID, acute attack x5–7 days)
Colchicine 1 mg then 0.5 mg after 1 h (acute attack, first 12 h)
Prednisolone 30–40 mg OD x5 days (if NSAIDs/colchicine contraindicated)
Allopurinol 100–300 mg OD (urate-lowering, start ≥ 4 weeks after acute attack resolves)
conditions.protocol: Acute: rapid anti-inflammatory (NSAID, colchicine, or steroid). Do NOT start allopurinol during acute attack. Urate-lowering: allopurinol titrated to serum uric acid < 360 μmol/L (or < 300 μmol/L if tophi).
conditions.contraindications: NSAIDs: avoid in renal impairment, peptic ulcer. Allopurinol: start low, titrate — risk of allopurinol hypersensitivity syndrome (HLA-B*5801 in Asian — consider screening).
conditions.notes: Dietary advice: limit red meat, shellfish, alcohol (especially beer). Adequate hydration. Review diuretics that may raise uric acid.

conditions.name: Melaena
drugs: Omeprazole 80 mg IV bolus then 8 mg/h infusion (if peptic ulcer suspected — pre-endoscopy)
Terlipressin 2 mg IV Q4H (if variceal bleed suspected)
Ceftriaxone 1 g IV OD (antibiotic prophylaxis for variceal bleed)
conditions.protocol: Resuscitate first: IV access x2, type and cross-match, fluid resuscitation. Urgent endoscopy within 24 h (12 h if high risk). Glasgow-Blatchford score to guide admission. NPO pending endoscopy.
conditions.contraindications: Avoid NSAIDs, anticoagulants until bleeding controlled.
conditions.notes: Monitor haemoglobin, haemodynamics closely. ICU/HDU if haemodynamically unstable. Surgical review if endoscopy fails.

conditions.name: Encounter for full-term uncomplicated delivery
drugs: Oxytocin 10 IU IM (active management of third stage — prevent PPH)
Ferrous sulphate 200 mg BD (post-delivery iron supplementation)
Metronidazole 400 mg TDS x5 days (if episiotomy infection)
conditions.protocol: Active management of third stage (oxytocin + cord traction). Perineal repair if episiotomy/tear. Monitor uterine tone, vital signs post-delivery. Encourage early breastfeeding.
conditions.contraindications: Oxytocin: avoid in foetal distress (before delivery), uterine scar if not monitored.
conditions.notes: Rhesus status — anti-D if Rh-negative mother. Postnatal depression screening. Contraception counselling before discharge.

conditions.name: Encounter for general adult medical examination
symptoms_keywords: kiểm tra sức khoẻ định kỳ
conditions.protocol: Review vitals, BMI, blood pressure. Screen: lipids, glucose, FBC, LFTs, renal function (age-appropriate). Cancer screening: cervical smear, mammogram, colonoscopy per guidelines. Vaccinations update.
conditions.notes: Document family history, social history, allergies. Lifestyle counselling: diet, exercise, smoking, alcohol. Mental health screen (PHQ-2, GAD-2).
